title: CI cost tiering: add Linux swift-format/SwiftLint cheap gate so macOS drops off the per-PR path
description: Follow-up to the ci.yml cost change (PR-gate macOS + weekly cron, dropped post-merge run, added concurrency/timeout). GOAL: take the 10x macOS build/test off the every-PR critical path by adding a 1x Linux cheap tier (swift-format lint + SwiftLint) that gates auto-merge, mirroring the iOS app repo's CHEAP/FULL tiering. BLOCKERS found 2026-06-25: (1) PassesCore imports Apple Compression -> kernel targets do NOT build on Linux, so the cheap tier can only LINT (syntactic), not compile. (2) Kernel has ~685 swift-format violations vs the app's .swift-format config and was never linted; SwiftLint app config needs its included: paths adapted to the kernel layout. PLAN: on a branch, (a) copy app .swift-format + .swiftlint.yml, adapt paths, (b) swift-format format --in-place + resolve SwiftLint, verify swift build/test still pass on macOS, (c) restructure ci.yml: Linux lint job (every PR, gates auto-merge) + macOS build/test moved to FULL (workflow_dispatch + weekly cron + full-ci label, draft-until-green for risky signature/parsing changes). NOTE: lint-only gating reduces merge safety on this security-critical kernel vs today's macOS-gated merges; the full-ci label/draft flow covers risky PRs. Do NOT commit the 685-line reformat straight to main.

comments.author: Cole Bittel
comments.text: Approach approved 2026-06-25: (1) swift-format format --in-place (685 mechanical), (2) swiftlint --fix (7 auto), (3) rename all 64 UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants to lowerCamelCase, (4) refactor remaining 8 (param-count -> param structs, force_cast, for_where, optional_data_string_conversion, cyclomatic_complexity test) with NO inline disables, (5) verify swift build+test (baseline: build OK, 470 tests pass), (6) restructure ci.yml into Linux cheap tier + full-ci.yml macOS. Working in worktree passes-ios-ipass-us8 / branch ios-ipass-us8-ci-cost-tiering.
